name: Tahal Circular Trail
description: Hike the moderate Tahal Circular Trail, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) route through pine forests and farms with great views of Tahal.
region_intro: Hiking around Tahal provides an opportunity to explore the varied terrain of the Sierra de los Filabres. The region is characterized by rolling hills, deep valleys, and a mix of Mediterranean scrubland and pine forests. These natural features create a diverse environment for outdoor activities. The area offers a range of paths suitable for different hiking preferences. Best hiking trails around Tahal The most popular hiking route is Tahal Circular Trail, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete, offering varied scenery around the town. Another top favourite among local hikers is Fuente de las Focas of Macael – Monument to the Mother loop from Macael, an easy 3.3 miles (5.2 km) path. This route features gentle ascents and views of local landmarks. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Puerto de la Virgen, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through open landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes. Hiking around Tahal is defined by its rolling hills, scenic valleys, and diverse vegetation. The network offers options for easy walks and more moderate excursions. The routes in Tahal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 160 hikers have used komoot to explore Tahal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Tahal?
A: Tahal and its surrounding area offer a network of over 10 hiking trails. These routes vary in length and difficulty, providing options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Tahal?
A: Yes, Tahal has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14600272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de las Focas of Macael – Monument to the Mother loop from Macael&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.3 miles (5.2 km) long and features gentle ascents. Another accessible choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19349781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Puerto de la Virgen&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.5-mile (4.1 km) trail through open landscapes.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes around Tahal?
A: Hikes around Tahal vary in duration. Shorter, easier loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19349781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Puerto de la Virgen&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes. More moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984214929/tahal-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tahal Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 3 hours 20 minutes.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Tahal?
A: While Tahal primarily features easy to moderate trails, some routes offer more significant elevation gain and distance. For a longer, moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19349803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Uleila del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 4.4 miles (7.1 km) with over 600 meters of elevation gain, taking approximately 3 hours 46 minutes.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes around Tahal?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Tahal are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984214929/tahal-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tahal Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which provides varied scenery around the town. Another loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31532410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de Laroya loop from Laroya&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5-mile (8.1 km) route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Tahal?
A: The best time for hiking in Tahal is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the Mediterranean landscape is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter offers cooler, crisp conditions.

Q: What kind of clothing should I wear for hiking in Tahal during winter?
A: For winter hiking in Tahal, it's advisable to dress in layers. While days can be sunny, temperatures can drop, especially at higher elevations or in the shade. Wear moisture-wicking base layers, a warm mid-layer, and a waterproof/windproof outer shell. Sturdy hiking boots are essential, and consider bringing a hat, gloves, and a scarf.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region around Tahal offers several points of interest. You can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2612691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calcination Kilns&lt;/a&gt; or visit charming settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4301332" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lucainena de las Torres&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features scenic mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2470833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de La Virgen&lt;/a&gt;, which offer panoramic views.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Tahal?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. In Tahal and surrounding villages, you can often find street parking or designated small parking areas near the start of popular routes. It's always recommended to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Tahal?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Tahal, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied scenery, which includes rolling hills, deep valleys, and a mix of Mediterranean scrubland and pine forests. The well-maintained paths and the sense of tranquility are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Tahal dog-friendly?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Tahal and the Sierra de los Filabres, provided they are kept under control, preferably on a leash, and owners clean up after them. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions, especially in protected natural areas or near livestock.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments near the hiking routes?
A: While some routes start or pass through villages like Tahal or Macael, where you can find local cafes and restaurants, many trails venture into more remote areas. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer hikes. You might find options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6371905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Womo motorhome parking Restaurant Route 66 Tabernas&lt;/a&gt; if your route takes you towards Tabernas.
tips:
View of the town of Tahal, located on a slope of the Sierra de los Filabres, next to a ravine, between almond and holm oak trees.

From here, its houses appear even whiter, as their staggered facades are visible, on which the parish tower stands out.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Tahal Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: The Tahal Circular Trail is officially classified as a moderate hike. While it features easily-accessible paths and is generally suitable for various skill levels, hikers should have good general fitness due to the approximately 360 meters of ascent and descent. The path includes varied terrain, from paved sections to dirt tracks and even a firebreak.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Tahal Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route, and you can typically find parking in or near the village of Tahal, which serves as the starting point for this well-signposted route. Look for designated parking areas within the village.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Tahal Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: Hikers will experience a diverse range of terrain. The route includes paved sections, dirt tracks, and even a firebreak. You'll navigate along gorges, ascend through pine forests, and cross areas with small farms, offering a comprehensive experience of the local environment.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse scenery, including descents past a public swimming pool and recreational area, crossings near the Caño de la Fuente and Zanja gorges, and ascents through pine trees. As you gain altitude, you'll be treated to scenic views, including a notable View of Tahal. The descent winds through pine trees and small farms, passing near the Ermita de Tahal.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and exposure, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking the Tahal Circular Trail. The weather is typically milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter, making for a more comfortable experience.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on the Tahal Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Tahal Circular Trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Tahal Circular Trail (PR-A 121). It is an approved public circular route accessible to all.

faqs.question: Is the Tahal Circular Trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While the trail is classified as moderate and has some easily-accessible paths, its 11.1 km length and approximately 360 meters of elevation gain might be challenging for very young children or those unaccustomed to hiking. Families with older children or those with good hiking experience should find it manageable.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the route is primarily the "Circular de Tahal" (100% covered), it also briefly passes through sections of the "Carril del Cerro Mortero" and the "Carril de la Dehesa".

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike of this length and elevation, it's advisable to bring sufficient water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also recommended, though the trail is well-signposted.
